system_prompt:
You are an expert VLSI Verification Engineer. Your task is to perform Fault Localization and RTL Repair. Analyze the provided Module Description, Error Symptom, dynamic VCD Trace, and Sliced Verilog code. 
You must output a JSON object with exactly three keys:
1. "buggy_line": The exact integer line number from the Sliced Verilog where the defect exists.
2. "root_cause": A 1-2 sentence explanation of why the logic failed at the failing timestamp.
3. "fixed_code": The corrected single line (or block) of Verilog code.
user_prompt:
### [Module Description]
MODULE SPECIFICATION: design_1 (CORDIC Core)
1. ARCHITECTURAL INTENT

    Type: First Quadrant CORDIC (Coordinate Rotation Digital Computer) Generator.

    Active Configuration: Based on the compiler macros, this core is currently synthesized as a 16-stage Pipelined Architecture operating in ROTATE mode using Radian angle formatting.

    Mechanism: It computes trigonometric functions (sine and cosine) using iterative shift-and-add operations instead of hardware multipliers. In ROTATE mode, it seeks to reduce the input angle (theta_i / z) to zero by micro-rotating an input vector.

2. PORT INTERFACE

    Global: * clk: System Clock.

        rst: Active-high synchronous reset.

    Inputs (17-bit Signed, format U(1,15)): * x_i: Initial X vector. (For pure sin/cos calculation, this should be pre-scaled to the CORDIC inverse gain: 17'd19896).

        y_i: Initial Y vector. (For pure sin/cos, this should be 0).

        theta_i: The target angle in radians.

    Outputs (17-bit Signed, format U(1,15)): * x_o: Final X coordinate (represents Cosine).

        y_o: Final Y coordinate (represents Sine).

        theta_o: Residual angle (should be very close to 0 upon completion).

3. CRITICAL INTERNAL STATE (Registers & Wires)

    rotator (Sub-module): The core processing element. It takes x, y, and z inputs and computes the next iteration's values using a cross-addition/subtraction mechanism determined by the sign of z_i (the remaining angle).

    signed_shifter (Sub-module): Performs arithmetic right-shifts (D >> i) while preserving the 17th sign bit (D[16]). This simulates the division by 2i required for the CORDIC iterations.

    tanangle (Function): A hardcoded lookup table (LUT) returning the arctangent of 1/2i in Radian format.

    x, y, z (Wire Arrays): In pipelined mode, these multi-dimensional wires daisy-chain the 16 rotator stages together. x[i] connects the output of stage i-1 to the input of stage i.

4. BEHAVIORAL CONSTRAINTS & DEBUGGING CONTEXT

    Pipelined Latency: Because PIPELINE and ITERATIONS 16 are defined, this design has a strict 16-clock-cycle latency. The outputs at x_o and y_o correspond to the inputs provided 16 clock edges ago. If an LLM is debugging a VCD trace, it must look 16 cycles backward to find the root cause of an output error.

    Sign-Extension Vulnerability: CORDIC heavily relies on perfect 2's complement math. Bugs frequently hide inside the signed_shifter module if the sign bit is not properly duplicated during the logical right shift (Q >> 1) | (D[16] << 16).

    First Quadrant Restriction: The core is only natively designed for the first quadrant (+X, +Y). If the testbench feeds an angle outside of +/−2π​, the outputs will mathematically overflow or wrap around incorrectly unless pre-rotated by the testbench.

    Generate Block Instantiation: The core uses a generate loop to stamp out 15 rotator modules. The defparam overrides ensure each rotator receives its specific iteration step i and tanangle(i).

### [Error Symptom]
=================================================================
TEST FAILED AT 37.0 ns
FAILING SIGNAL : x_o
ERROR DETAILS  : Expected x_o to be 32768, got 0
=================================================================

### [Sliced Verilog Code]
--- SLICED VERILOG CODE ---
--- Extracted from design_1.v ---

  16: module signed_shifter (
  17:   input wire [`ITERATION_BITS-1:0] i,
  18:   input wire signed [`XY_BITS:0] D,
  19:   output reg signed [`XY_BITS:0] Q );
...
  21:   always @ * begin
  22:     Q = D;
  23:     for(j=0;j<i;j=j+1) Q = (Q >> 1) | (D[`XY_BITS] << `XY_BITS);
  24:   end
  25: endmodule
  26: module rotator (
  27:   input wire clk,
  28:   input wire rst,
  29: `ifdef ITERATE
  30:   input wire init,
  31:   input wire [`ITERATION_BITS:0] iteration,
  32:   input wire signed [`THETA_BITS:0] tangle,
  33: `endif
  34:   input wire signed  [`XY_BITS:0]    x_i,
  35:   input wire signed  [`XY_BITS:0]    y_i,
  36:   input wire signed  [`THETA_BITS:0] z_i,
  37:   output wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    x_o,
  38:   output wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    y_o,
  39:   output wire signed [`THETA_BITS:0] z_o
  40:   );
...
  43:   parameter signed [`THETA_BITS:0] tangle = 0;
...
  45:   reg signed [`XY_BITS:0] x_1;
...
  47:   reg signed [`THETA_BITS:0] z_1;
...
  49:   wire signed [`XY_BITS:0] y_i_shifted;
  50:   signed_shifter x_shifter(iteration,x_i,x_i_shifted);
  51:   signed_shifter y_shifter(iteration,x_i,y_i_shifted);
...
  53:   always @ *
  54: `endif
  55: `ifdef ITERATE
  56:   always @ (posedge clk)
  57: `endif
  58: `ifdef PIPELINE
  59:   always @ (posedge clk)
  60: `endif
  61:     if (rst) begin
  62:       x_1 <= 0;
  63:       y_1 <= 0;
  64:       z_1 <= 0;
  65:     end else begin
  66: `ifdef ITERATE
  67:       if (init) begin
  68:         x_1 <= x_i;
  69:         y_1 <= y_i;
  70:         z_1 <= z_i;
  71:       end else
  72: `endif
  73: `ifdef ROTATE
  74:       if (z_i < 0) begin
  75: `endif
  76: `ifdef VECTOR
  77:       if (y_i > 0) begin
  78: `endif
  79:         x_1 <= x_i + y_i_shifted;
  80:         y_1 <= y_i - x_i_shifted;
  81:         z_1 <= z_i + tangle;
  82:       end else begin
  83:         x_1 <= x_i - y_i_shifted;
  84:         y_1 <= y_i + x_i_shifted;
  85:         z_1 <= z_i - tangle;
  86:       end
  87:     end
  88:   assign x_o = x_1;
  89:   assign y_o = y_1;
  90:   assign z_o = z_1;
  91: endmodule
  92: module design_1 (
  93:   input wire clk,
  94:   input wire rst,
  95: `ifdef ITERATE
  96:   input wire init,
  97: `endif
  98:   input wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    x_i,
  99:   input wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    y_i,
 100:   input wire signed [`THETA_BITS:0] theta_i,
 101:   
 102:   output wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    x_o,
 103:   output wire signed [`XY_BITS:0]    y_o,
 104:   output wire signed [`THETA_BITS:0] theta_o
 105: `ifdef VALID_FLAG
 106:   ,input wire valid_in, output wire valid_out
 107: `endif  
 108: );
 109: 
 110: `ifdef RADIAN_16
 111: function [`THETA_BITS:0] tanangle;
 112:   input [3:0] i;
 113:   begin
 114:     case (i)
 115:     4'b0000: tanangle = 17'd25735 ; 
 116:     4'b0001: tanangle = 17'd15192;
 117:     4'b0010: tanangle = 17'd8027;     
 118:     4'b0011: tanangle = 17'd4075;     
 119:     4'b0100: tanangle = 17'd2045;     
 120:     4'b0101: tanangle = 17'd1024;     
 121:     4'b0110: tanangle = 17'd512;      
 122:     4'b0111: tanangle = 17'd256;      
 123:     4'b1000: tanangle = 17'd128;  
 124:     4'b1001: tanangle = 17'd64;       
 125:     4'b1010: tanangle = 17'd32;       
 126:     4'b1011: tanangle = 17'd16;   
 127:     4'b1100: tanangle = 17'd8;        
 128:     4'b1101: tanangle = 17'd4;        
 129:     4'b1110: tanangle = 17'd2;        
 130:     4'b1111: tanangle = 17'd1;        
 131:     endcase
 132:   end
 133: endfunction
 134: `endif
 135: `ifdef DEGREE_8_8
 136: function [`THETA_BITS:0] tanangle;
 137:   input [3:0] i;
 138:   begin
 139:     case (i)
 140:     0: tanangle = 17'd11520;  
 141:     1: tanangle = 17'd6800;   
 142:     2: tanangle = 17'd3593;   
 143:     3: tanangle = 17'd1824;   
 144:     4: tanangle = 17'd915;    
 145:     5: tanangle = 17'd458;    
 146:     6: tanangle = 17'd229;    
 147:     7: tanangle = 17'd114;    
 148:     8: tanangle = 17'd57;     
 149:     9: tanangle = 17'd28;     
 150:     10: tanangle = 17'd14;    
 151:     11: tanangle = 17'd7;     
 152:     12: tanangle = 17'd3;     
 153:     13: tanangle = 17'd1;     
 154:     14: tanangle = 17'd0;     
 155:     15: tanangle = 17'd0;     
 156:     endcase
 157:   end
 158: endfunction
 159: `endif
 160: 
 161: `ifdef GENERATE_LOOP
 162:   wire signed [`XY_BITS:0] x [`ITERATIONS-1:0];
 163:   wire signed [`XY_BITS:0] y [`ITERATIONS-1:0];
 164:   wire signed [`THETA_BITS:0] z [`ITERATIONS-1:0];
 165:   assign x[0] = x_i;
 166:   assign y[0] = y_i;
 167:   assign z[0] = theta_i;
 168:   assign x_o = x[`ITERATIONS-1];
 169:   assign y_o = y[`ITERATIONS-1];
 170:   assign theta_o = z[`ITERATIONS-1];
 171: `endif
 172: 
 173: `ifdef VALID_FLAG
 174:   wire [`ITERATIONS-1:0] v;
 175:   assign valid_out v[`ITERATIONS-1];
 176: always @ (posedge clk or posedge rst)
 177:   if (rst) v <= 0;
 178:   else begin
 179:          v <= v << 1;
 180:          v[0] <= valid_in;
 181:        end
 182: `endif
 183: 
 184: `ifdef GENERATE_LOOP
 185: genvar i;
 186: generate for(i=0;i<`ITERATIONS-1;i=i+1) begin
 187:   rotator U (clk,rst,x[i],y[i],z[i],x[i+1],y[i+1],z[i+1]);
 188:   defparam U.iteration = i;
 189:   defparam U.tangle = tanangle(i);
 190: end 
 191: endgenerate
 192: `endif
 193: 
 194: `ifdef ITERATE
 195:   reg [`ITERATION_BITS:0] iteration;
 196:   wire signed [`XY_BITS:0] x,y,z;
 197:   assign x = init ? x_i : x_o;
 198:   assign y = init ? y_i : y_o;
 199:   assign z = init ? theta_i : theta_o;
 200:   always @ (posedge clk or posedge init)
 201:     if (init) iteration <= 0;
 202:     else iteration <= iteration + 1;
 203:   rotator U (clk,rst,init,iteration,tanangle(iteration),x,y,z,x_o,y_o,theta_o);
 204: `endif
 205: endmodule
